titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the New England Church Records
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Martha Lund Smalley, James M. Starr, and Carl L. Beckwith

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 29, 2020 at 22:00

did.abstract: Record Group 48 is an open collection composed of records from individual churches in the six New England states: Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, and Vermont.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: New England Church Records (RG 48), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Christians first arrived in the area that would become New England in the early 1600s. The first Christian settlers were predominantly English Puritans, and members of other denominations arrived over the following decades. The churches represented in this collection are predominantly Congregational, with other denominations - including Episcopal, Presbyterian, Lutheran, Methodist, Unitarian/Universalist, and others - also represented.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A small portion of the material is in manuscript form while the majority is printed, including manuals, directories, news bulletins and historical papers. Additional documentation regarding these churches may be available in the Library's cataloged collection.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The churches are arranged alphabetically within each state with name changes and other historical data noted when pertinent. The material for each church is arranged chronologically except in a few cases where the quantity of material warranted a more detailed arrangement.

subject.part: Church records and registers -- New England
Congregational churches -- New England -- History -- Sources
Lutheran Church -- New England -- History -- Sources
Methodist Church -- New England -- History -- Sources
Presbyterian Church -- New England -- History -- Sources

corpname.part: Catholic Church -- History -- Sources -- New England
Episcopal Church -- History -- Sources -- New England
Methodist Episcopal Church -- History -- Sources -- New England
United Church on the Green (New Haven, Conn.)
United Church of Christ -- History -- Sources -- New England

did.unittitle: Spring Glen Church, United Church of Christ (UCC)
Benedict Memorial Church (see: First Presbyterian Church)
Chapel of St. Thomas More at Yale

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Named changed in 1871 to Church of the Redeemer - see Record Group 266

did.unittitle: Church of Christ in Yale University

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Formerly known as Chapel St. Congregational Church - see Record Group 266

did.unittitle: Church Street Church (see also Plymouth Congregational Church)
College Street Congregational Church (see also Plymouth Congregational Church)
Dixwell Avenue Congregational Church / United Church of Christ
First Congregational Church in Fair Haven (see also Grand Avenue Congregational Church)
First Church of Christ in New Haven (also known as Center Church on the Green)
Booklets and bulletins re: history of church and its meeting house
Correspondence regarding hiring of assistant pastor
Reports of Ladies' Home Missionary Society
Organizations and programs, including First Church Home for Aged Women
Refugee Resettlement Project (Cambodia refugees sponsored by church)
Constitution of Young People's Christian Endeavor Society
Notes on History of Church Gleaned from Session Minutes
Financial Record Book of Women's Groups
First Unitarian Universalist Society of New Haven
Books by Phebe Hanaford: Daughters of America; or Women of the Century (1882) and From Shore to Shore and Other Poems (1871)
A Collection of Psalms and Hymns for the Use of Universalist Societies and Families by Hosea Ballou
Bound volume: "Record of the First Universalist Society" (1836-1870) (holograph)
Bound volume: Records of the Prudential Committee, First Universalist Society (1870-1895) (holograph)
Bound volume: Records of the Social Service Committee of Men's League, First Universalist Society (holograph)
Looseleaf notebook: transcripts and copies of historical documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including: "Records of the First Universalist Society in New Haven (1836) (20 typed pages); "Historical Sketch of the First Universalist Church and Society in New Haven, Conn. (1873) by Phebe Hanaford (20 page pamphlet); "The History of the First Universalist Church" by Lucy Creemer Peckham (1911) (6 typed pages); "History of the Society" compile by Eunice K. Armstead (1936) (1 page); "The Finished Work; of Four Years in New Haven" a farewell sermon by Phebe Hanaford (1874) (14 page typed transcript); Additional histories, list of places of worship, list of ministers.

did.unittitle: Free Congregational Church (see also Plymouth Congregational Church)
Grand Avenue Congregational Church (see also First Congregational Church in Fair Haven)

bibliography.head: Bibliography
bibliography.p: North Church united with Third Congregational Church in 1884 to form the United Church. North Church also referred to as the United Society.

did.unittitle: Plymouth Congregational Church (name changes: 1831-Missionary Church, 1832-Free Congregational Church, 1839-Church St. Church, 1848-College St. Church, 1895-Plymouth Congregational Church.)
St. John Street Methodist Episcopal Church
Second Congregational Church of Fair Haven
Third Congregational Church (Third Church united with North Church in 1884 to form United Church): General material
United Church of New Haven (Formed in 1884 by union of North and Third Churches): General Materials
UCNH: re: Joint Sunday School with Center Church
Center Congregational Church (also known as Third Congregational or First Church of Christ)
Church of St. John the Baptist
Church of Christ in Amherst College
Brattle Square Church (also known as Brattle Street Church)
New South Free Church and Society
First Trinity Congregational Church of Christ
